title: Resize landing page logo and add YouTube link to CMS

requirements.summary: Reduce the game logo size on the landing page so it no longer dominates the desktop viewport while maintaining responsive behavior
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, the game logo is visibly smaller than in the previous version and does not dominate the viewport
The logo scales down fluidly on mobile and tablet without cropping or overflow
max-width: 100% and auto height are still applied so the logo never breaks the layout on any screen size
Dark mode inversion (CSS filter invert) is preserved on the logo

file_operations.description: Update the game logo image styling to reduce its desktop maximum width from full natural width to 480px–560px. Keep max-width: 100% and height: auto for responsive behavior. Preserve the dark mode invert filter. The logo should appear noticeably smaller on desktop while maintaining fluid scaling on smaller screens.

requirements.summary: Add a YouTube Link field to the Admin Dashboard CMS so admins can edit the video link used in the press kit
acceptanceCriteria: The Admin Dashboard shows a clearly labelled 'YouTube Link' text input field in the CMS section
The input is pre-populated with the current youtubeLink value from the backend
Clicking Save calls updateYoutubeLink() with the new value
After a successful save, the content query is invalidated and refetched so the press kit reflects the updated link immediately
A success or error state is shown to the admin after saving

file_operations.description: Add a useUpdateYoutubeLink mutation hook that calls actor.updateYoutubeLink(link) and invalidates the 'content' query on success. Follow the existing mutation pattern used for updateAbout, updateFeatures, etc.

Add a YouTube Link text input field to the CMS form section. Pre-populate it with content.youtubeLink from the content query. Wire up a Save button that calls the useUpdateYoutubeLink mutation with the input value. Display success/error feedback using toast or inline messages after save. Ensure the field is clearly labeled and positioned logically with other CMS fields.

requirements.summary: Update the VideoSection component to use the admin-editable youtubeLink from the CMS instead of a hardcoded URL
acceptanceCriteria: The VideoSection component receives the youtubeLink from the CMS content query rather than a hardcoded string
Clicking the video thumbnail replaces it with a YouTube iframe using the admin-set youtubeLink
If youtubeLink is empty or not yet set, the component falls back gracefully (e.g., the play button does nothing or is hidden)

file_operations.description: Update VideoSection to accept a youtubeLink prop. Replace any hardcoded YouTube URL with this prop value when constructing the iframe src. Add graceful fallback handling if youtubeLink is empty (disable the play button or hide the video section).
Pass content.youtubeLink from the content query as a prop to the VideoSection component. Ensure the component receives the dynamic link so it updates when the admin changes it in the CMS.
